Brian Cho: Thanks all.

meeting_title: Aegis / Harborview Banking - Threat Detection Tuning
chunk_summary: Introductions and agenda setting for threat detection tuning session.
chunk_text:
Lisa Park: Alright, I think we've got everyone on now — Patricia, Michael, can you both hear us okay?
Patricia Dunn: Yep, coming through fine on my end.
Michael Russo: Good here as well.
Lisa Park: Perfect, great. So, um, welcome everyone — this is our threat detection tuning session for Harborview Banking. I'm Lisa Park, account manager on the Aegis side, and I've got Kevin O'Brien with me today, he's our solutions architect who's going to be doing most of the heavy lifting on the technical side of things.
Kevin O'Brien: Hey everyone, Kevin here. Looking forward to getting into the weeds on the Detect configuration with you all today.
Patricia Dunn: Thanks for having us. I'm Patricia Dunn, VP of Security here at Harborview. And I've got Michael Russo on with me, he's our compliance officer — we brought him in because a lot of what we're trying to tune around has some compliance implications that we want to make sure we're capturing.
Kevin O'Brien: That makes a lot of sense, actually. Good thinking bringing Michael in. We find that the compliance angle and the detection angle overlap more than people expect, especially in financial services.
Michael Russo: Yeah, exactly. That's kind of been our experience too over the years. You get siloed and then you're doing double the work.
Lisa Park: Totally. So, um, before we jump in — Kevin, do you want to just set the agenda for today real quick so Patricia and Michael know what to expect?
Kevin O'Brien: Sure, yeah. So the plan for today is — we want to walk through your current Aegis Detect configuration, understand what alert thresholds you have set, talk about any false positive pain points you're running into, and then start mapping out what a tuned detection profile looks like for a financial services environment like yours. We'll also touch on your event source integrations, because that's usually where a lot of the noise comes from in the early onboarding phase.
embedding_text: Session Introduction. Introductions and agenda setting for threat detection tuning session.
sentiment_reason: Neutral tone during introductions and agenda overview.

meeting_title: Weekly Engineering Standup
chunk_summary: Recap of action items and priorities for the upcoming week.

Ravi Gupta: Yeah, fair enough. I'll have it updated by end of day.
Raj Kapoor: Perfect. Okay, I think we're at about time. Any other blockers before we wrap?
Sofia Petrov: Nothing from me.
Ravi Gupta: Same. I'm good.
Raj Kapoor: Alright. To summarize — Comply timeout fix is Sofia's priority, Ravi assists with QA. Circuit breaker PR gets the health check added today, then Sofia reviews and we merge. Runbook gets finished this week. And load testing goes on the roadmap for April 28th start. I'll send a recap in Slack. Good work, everyone — talk next week.
embedding_text: Action Item Recap. Recap of action items and priorities for the upcoming week.
sentiment_reason: Summary of tasks and commitments without emotional content.

meeting_title: Aegis / Crestline Wealth - Identity Module Deployment
chunk_summary: Introductions and discussion of current identity management challenges.
chunk_text:
Rachel Torres: Alright, I think we've got everyone on — Derek, can you hear us okay?
Derek Owens: Yeah, loud and clear. Good to go.
Rachel Torres: Perfect. Okay so, welcome everyone — I'm Rachel Torres, Senior Account Manager here at Aegis, and I have Sofia Petrov with me today, she's one of our Senior Engineers on the Identity side of things. Sofia, you wanna say a quick hi?
Sofia Petrov: Hey Derek, great to meet you. Really excited to be working with Crestline on this — Identity deployments in financial services are kind of my favorite thing to dig into, so, yeah, looking forward to the conversation.
Derek Owens: Likewise, honestly. I've been looking forward to this one. We've had Protect and Detect running for a while now and the team's been happy, so adding Identity feels like a natural next step.
Rachel Torres: That's great to hear, and yeah, that's — that's kind of the pattern we see with a lot of our financial services customers. They start with backup and threat monitoring and then once Identity is on the roadmap it sort of becomes a priority real fast, especially given the regulatory environment you're dealing with.
Derek Owens: Exactly, yeah. We had our annual audit in February and the auditors flagged some gaps around privileged access management and MFA coverage across our admin accounts specifically. So that sort of lit a fire under us to get this moving.
embedding_text: Introduction and Context. Introductions and discussion of current identity management challenges.
sentiment_reason: Participants express enthusiasm and share relevant context about identity management.

meeting_title: Aegis / Frostbyte AI - Product Feedback
chunk_summary: Introductions and context setting for the discussion on Aegis Identity.
chunk_text:
Rachel Torres: Hey Victor, thanks for making time today — I know your calendar's been pretty packed lately.
Victor Zheng: Yeah, no worries, it's been a busy quarter but this is — this is on my list, so happy to make it happen.
Rachel Torres: Good, good. So I've also got Sofia Petrov on with me today, she's one of our senior engineers on the Identity side, and I figured having her here would be more useful than me trying to take notes and relay everything back.
Sofia Petrov: Hey Victor, thanks for having us. Really looking forward to hearing what you've been running into.
Victor Zheng: Hey Sofia, yeah, appreciate you jumping on. Okay so — where do I start. So we've been on Aegis Identity now for, I want to say, about seven months? Maybe closer to eight.
Rachel Torres: Yeah, that tracks, I think you all went live around mid-September last year.
Victor Zheng: Right, yeah. So overall — look, it's been fine. The SSO integration was pretty smooth once we got through the initial SAML configuration, and the MFA rollout went better than I expected honestly.
Sofia Petrov: That's good to hear. That SAML piece can be... yeah, it can be a little finicky depending on the IdP.
Victor Zheng: It was, yeah, we had a couple of back-and-forths with your support team on that, but it got resolved. So no complaints there. The reason I wanted to have this call is really around some gaps we're hitting on the IAM side — specifically around how Aegis Identity handles role management at scale.
embedding_text: Meeting Introduction. Introductions and context setting for the discussion on Aegis Identity.
sentiment_reason: Positive tone in greetings and acknowledgment of busy schedules.

meeting_title: Aegis / Bridgeport Health - Compliance Review
chunk_summary: Meeting concludes with positive feedback and appreciation for progress.
chunk_text:
Maria Santos: I love that framing. Okay, so let me think about next steps — I'll follow up with you on scheduling that Comply v2 preview, and Kevin, can you send over a framework mapping document that shows the SOC 2 to HIPAA control overlap? Something Evelyn's team can actually use as a working guide.
Kevin O'Brien: Yeah, for sure. I'll put together a healthcare-specific version of that, pull in some of the PHI-related controls specifically. I'll have it to Maria by end of next week.
Evelyn Park: That would be incredibly helpful. My compliance lead, she's going to be very happy when I forward that to her.
Maria Santos: And one more thing — Evelyn, you mentioned PCI earlier, or actually I think it was... wait, no, you didn't mention PCI. Sorry, I was thinking of another customer. Are PCI requirements on your radar at all or is it really just HIPAA and SOC 2?
Evelyn Park: No, PCI isn't really relevant for us on the clinical side. We do have some billing systems but those go through a third-party processor so we're mostly scoped out. HIPAA and SOC 2 are the big two.
Maria Santos: Got it, good to know. Okay, I think we've covered a lot of ground today. Evelyn, any other questions or concerns you want to make sure we address before we wrap up?
Evelyn Park: Honestly, no — I feel really good about where this is heading. I was a little nervous going into this call because Q2 feels like it's sneaking up on me, but talking through it, I think we've got a solid plan. I really appreciate you both making time.
Maria Santos: Of course! That's what we're here for. And Evelyn, seriously, your team has done great work getting to where you are with the platform in eight months. A lot of organizations take much longer to get to this point.
Evelyn Park: Thank you, that means a lot. Alright, I'll keep an eye out for Kevin's framework doc and the preview session invite. Looking forward to it.
Maria Santos: Perfect. We'll be in touch soon. Thanks everyone, take care!
embedding_text: Wrap-Up and Acknowledgments. Meeting concludes with positive feedback and appreciation for progress.
sentiment_reason: Customer feels reassured and appreciates the support provided.

chunk_summary: Introductions and overview of support case 1750 regarding SIEM connector issues.
chunk_text:
Sarah Chen: Hi, good afternoon, this is Sarah Chen from Aegis Cloud Security support, I have Dr. Cheng and Mr. Burke on the line from Vanta Health Systems, and my colleague Priya Patel is joining as well — Priya, you there?
Priya Patel: Yeah, I'm here, hi everyone.
Dr. Linda Cheng: Hello, yes, Dr. Linda Cheng here, and Raymond is with me as well.
Raymond Burke: Raymond Burke, yeah, hi, thanks for getting us on quickly.
Sarah Chen: Of course, so I've had a chance to look at your case notes, Support Case 1750, and I understand you're seeing issues with your SIEM connector in Aegis Detect — can you walk us through what you're experiencing from your end?
embedding_text: Meeting Introduction. Introductions and overview of support case 1750 regarding SIEM connector issues.
sentiment_reason: Standard greetings and case context setting.

meeting_title: Support Case #6147 - Summit Trust Detect Alert Delays
chunk_summary: Confirmation of follow-up actions and monitoring for alert latency.
chunk_text:
Alicia Monroe: I think that covers it for now. We'll be watching our alert latency closely and we expect to hear from you.
David Kim: You will. Thank you both for your time, I'm sorry this happened, and we'll get it sorted. Take care.
embedding_text: Follow-Up Actions. Confirmation of follow-up actions and monitoring for alert latency.
sentiment_reason: Meeting concludes with agreement on next steps and monitoring.

meeting_title: Protect Performance - Scalability Concerns
chunk_summary: Participants confirm audio and agent introduces the meeting purpose.
chunk_text:
Chris Lee: Alright, I think we're all on — can everyone hear me okay?
Nina Kowalski: Yeah, I can hear you.
Tyler Washington: Yep, good here.
Sofia Petrov: Same, I'm on.
Chris Lee: Okay good, so — let's just dive in because I don't want this to run long. The reason I called this is, uh, we've been getting some pretty alarming signals from the Protect module over the last couple of weeks and I think we need to talk through what's actually happening before this becomes a customer-facing issue.
embedding_text: Meeting Introduction. Participants confirm audio and agent introduces the meeting purpose.
sentiment_reason: Initial greetings and agenda setting.

meeting_title: Aegis / Atlas Precision - Annual Review & Renewal
chunk_summary: Summary of action items and positive closing remarks.
chunk_text:
Wayne Keller: Sure, I can pull together some notes on that. It's probably a conversation in itself.
Daniel Okafor: Perfect. Alright, I think we're in really good shape here. To summarize — proposal comes to you this week, flat pricing on current modules, separate call with Priya on Identity, and Mike will loop you in on the OT roadmap details when they're cleared. Does that cover everything from your side?
Wayne Keller: Yeah, I think that's it. This was a good call — I feel good about where things are heading. Appreciate you guys taking the time.
Daniel Okafor: Always, Wayne. We appreciate Atlas Precision — seriously. Have a good rest of your week and enjoy the garden weather while it lasts.
Wayne Keller: Ha, yeah. Thanks guys, talk soon.
embedding_text: Meeting Wrap-Up. Summary of action items and positive closing remarks.
sentiment_reason: Customer expresses satisfaction with the meeting outcomes.

meeting_title: SOC 2 Type II - Final Review
chunk_summary: Recognizing team efforts in the audit preparation process.
chunk_text:
Nina Kowalski: Well, and that's honestly a credit to everyone on this call. Ravi, you've been driving this thing relentlessly, Tyler the technical documentation is rock solid, Nina your QA process caught things we would have been embarrassed to submit. It's just — yeah, it's been a good team effort.
Ravi Gupta: Aw, thanks Nina. Okay, I think we've covered everything — let me just recap the actions real quick: Nina signs off by Wednesday, I finalize Thursday morning, Tyler you're on standby for any last-minute evidence questions, and Diana you'll hold comms with the auditor contact. Everyone good?
Tyler Washington: Good with me.
Nina Kowalski: Yep, sounds great. Excited to get this across the finish line.
Diana Reeves: Same. Thanks everyone, this was a great call.
embedding_text: Team Acknowledgment. Recognizing team efforts in the audit preparation process.
sentiment_reason: Team expresses appreciation for collaborative efforts.
